headline: Major California American Water Infrastructure Investment is Completed in Carmel-By-The-Sea
summary: The completion of a new underground water main pipe project in the City of Carmel-By-The-Sea marks an important investment in local infrastructure, helping improve long-term water service reliability and support the community's future needs.

raw.headline: Major California American Water Infrastructure Investment is Completed in Carmel-By-The-Sea

headline: Virginia American Water Reaches Settlement Agreement in General Rate Case
summary: On June 5, Virginia American Water filed with the Virginia State Corporation Commission (the "SCC") a settlement agreement for its current statewide General Rate Case. The filing memorializes the settlement agreement reached with the staff of the SCC and several intervenors and remains subject to SCC review and approval.

headline: California American Water Reaches Partial Settlement Agreement in General Rate Case
summary: Yesterday, California American Water filed with the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) a partial settlement agreement for its statewide General Rate Case to set revenue for 2027 to 2029. The filing memorializes the settlement agreement reached with the CPUC's Public Advocates Office. The remaining issue regarding treatment of Construction Work in Progress (CWIP) is pending.

headline: American Water State Presidents Help Advance Solutions for Water Reliability and Affordability at MARC 2026
summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK), the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S., contributed expertise to key discussions at the Mid-America Regulatory Conference (MARC) 2026 held June 7-10, 2026 in Madison, Wis. headline: American Water Grants Highlight Community Impact And Valuation Context For AWK Investors
summary: American Water Charitable Foundation and Tennessee American Water awarded major environmental grants to local community organizations. The funding supports projects focused on water quality, conservation, and environmental education in the communities the company serves. The initiative reflects American Water Works Company (NYSE:AWK) using philanthropic programs alongside its core regulated utility operations.
